summary: Official policy control for Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting. The local Microsoft GameDVR.admx file explicitly documents the same GameDVR policy key, AllowGameDVR value name, and disable behavior that the app uses.

setting.name: Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting
setting.casual_explanation: Windows includes built-in game recording and broadcasting features. This policy decides whether those features are allowed on the device.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they do not use Windows game capture features or want to keep gaming overlays and background recording options out of the system.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off can reduce clutter and disable built-in recording behavior, but users lose native Windows game capture and broadcasting features.
setting.professional_notes: GameDVR.admx maps AllowGameDVR under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. GameDVR.adml says disabling the policy prevents Windows Game Recording, while enabling it or leaving it not configured allows recording and broadcasting. Microsoft also publishes the same policy in the ApplicationManagement Policy CSP.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps its normal game recording and broadcasting baseline.

allowed_values.label: Game recording disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting are not allowed by policy.

allowed_values.label: Game recording allowed
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting are allowed.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowGameDVR = 0, which matches the documented disabled policy state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client systems that expose the GameDVR policy but do not configure it
windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents that enabling or not configuring the policy allows the feature.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Gamers who want built-in Windows recording available
avoid_for: Systems that should not allow Windows game capture features
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal game recording baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the built-in recording and broadcasting feature disabled by policy.
tradeoffs: The feature stays closer to the normal Windows baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: No built-in game recording
intended_for: Systems that do not use Windows game capture
avoid_for: Users who rely on native Windows recording or broadcasting

states.rationale: Disable Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not want Windows' built-in game recording and broadcasting features available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use the built-in Xbox Game Bar capture flow and want it left available.
tradeoffs: Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting are disabled by policy.
Users lose the built-in recording feature.

evidence.title: Microsoft ApplicationManagement Policy CSP: AllowGameDVR
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the policy that controls Windows Game Recording and Broadcasting under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR\AllowGameDVR.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GameDVR.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps AllowGameDVR to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR\AllowGameDVR with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GameDVR.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says disabling the policy prevents Windows Game Recording, while enabling it or leaving it not configured allows recording and broadcasting.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowGameDVR = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft GameDVR.admx file explicitly defines AllowGameDVR under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR with valueName AllowGameDVR, and the paired ADML help text states that disabling the setting prevents Windows Game Recording while enabled or not configured allows recording and broadcasting. The app writes that documented disabled state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same GameDVR policy key and AllowGameDVR value used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that disabling the setting prevents Windows Game Recording, while enabled or not configured allows recording and broadcasting.
